cot_prefix: Okay, let's read through the text to find out what upset Melanie during the National Championships. First, the question is asking about an event that happened during the National Championships that upset her. Let me find the relevant part of the text. Looking at the paragraph where Melanie talks about her experience in the National Championships: "Melanie entered her first triathlon at 14 and she won the junior section. Full of confidence, she entered the National Championships, and although she had the second fastest swim and the fastest run, she came nowhere. 'I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running.'" So, the key points here are that Melanie was following someone and took a wrong turn, leading her to cycle 20 km too far. This mistake caused her to not perform well in the race, and she ended up crying during the running part. The question asks what upset her during the National Championships, and the answer seems to be that she made a mistake during part of the race (the cycling part, going too far). Looking at the options: (A) She was tricked by another competitor. – The text doesn't mention her being tricked; she was following someone who also went wrong. It doesn't seem like a deliberate trick to me. So probably not A. (B) She felt she had let her teammates down. – The text doesn't mention her teammates in the context of this race. She's talking about her own experience here. So B is unlikely. (C) She made a mistake during part of the race. – This aligns with the event where she went the wrong way and cycled 20 km too far. So C
